headline: Donkeys protect Spanish national park from wildfires for 9 years
meta_description: Eighteen donkeys have kept Doñana National Park fire-free for nine years by grazing on dry vegetation in hard-to-reach areas.
summary_short: Eighteen grazing donkeys have protected a vital Spanish nature reserve from wildfires for nine consecutive years.
body:
In Doñana National Park, one of Europe's most important natural reserves, an unconventional approach to wildfire prevention has proven remarkably effective: eighteen donkeys. By grazing on dry scrub in areas vehicles cannot safely reach, the donkeys have kept the park fire-free for nine straight years.

The strategy works because dry vegetation is removed before it can accumulate into kindling for wildfires. It's low-cost, low-impact, and works with nature rather than against it. The donkeys maintain the landscape while protecting the park's extraordinary biodiversity. The success demonstrates that traditional and innovative solutions—animal grazing paired with modern conservation—can protect precious ecosystems in ways that industrial methods sometimes cannot.
